message:
Running command: 
# DRT2 at 60K groups / 13m56s CPU. Python drainer working!
# Monitor with long waits - DRT2 should take same time as DRT1 (~90min elapsed)
# Let's wait 90 minutes and check status

for i in $(seq 1 180); do
    sleep 30
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] OpenROAD exited!" && break
    
    SYSCALL=$(cat /proc/$ORPID/syscall 2>/dev/null | head -c10)
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    PIPEBYTES=$(wc -c < /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null)
    LATESTMSG=$(tail -2 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')
    
    RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "YES" || echo "no")
    LATEST_STEP=$(ls "$RUN_DIR/" | sort -V | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -1)
    
    echo "[$i $(date +%H:%M:%S)] SYS=$SYSCALL CPU=$CPUTIME PIPE=${PIPEBYTES}B FINAL=$FINAL STEP=$LATEST_STEP MSG=$LATESTMSG"
    
    [ "$FINAL" = "YES" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break
done

function.name: execute_bash

args.command:
# DRT2 at 60K groups / 13m56s CPU. Python drainer working!
# Monitor with long waits - DRT2 should take same time as DRT1 (~90min elapsed)
# Let's wait 90 minutes and check status

for i in $(seq 1 180); do
    sleep 30
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] OpenROAD exited!" && break
    
    SYSCALL=$(cat /proc/$ORPID/syscall 2>/dev/null | head -c10)
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    PIPEBYTES=$(wc -c < /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null)
    LATESTMSG=$(tail -2 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')
    
    RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "YES" || echo "no")
    LATEST_STEP=$(ls "$RUN_DIR/" | sort -V | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -1)
    
    echo "[$i $(date +%H:%M:%S)] SYS=$SYSCALL CPU=$CPUTIME PIPE=${PIPEBYTES}B FINAL=$FINAL STEP=$LATEST_STEP MSG=$LATESTMSG"
    
    [ "$FINAL" = "YES" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break
done
